results.rendered_html: haṃho ind. (cf. ham) a vocative particle (corresponding to ‘ho!’ ‘hollo!’ accord. to some also expressing haughtiness or contempt; in dram. a form of address used by equals to each other),  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
haṃho ind.
1 A vocative particle
(pb) corresponding to, ‘ho,’ ‘hallo’; haṃho cinmayacittacaṃdramaṇayaḥ saṃvardhayadhvaṃ rasān  Chandr. 1. 2. 
2 A particle expressing haughtiness or contempt.
3 A particle of interrogation. (In dramas it is mostly used as a form of address by characters of the middling class; haṃho bāhmaṇa mā kuṣya  Mu. 1 ).
